items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: {PlayerName}—over here!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: You bet! Just passed all my entrance exams yesterday!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Which means... today you're officially meeting "Lynae, first-year student in Startorch Academy"!

options.text: So when's our next joyride going to be?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Ugh, I really wanna hit the road with you again, but I'm kinda on a "mental health watchlist." If I break even one tiny rule on day one, Miss Lucilla will be dragging me in for a "friendly chat."

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I gotta hold out until I figure out a route that slips past the cameras.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Oh, but hey, you got Mornye's email about the <te href=700013>Hyvatia Detector</te>, right? It's almost go-time. How about we hang by the entrance, and then we—

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Material fatigue... thermal cycles... boundary conditions... initial stress states...

items.speaker: "TARD-E Unit"
items.text: Infraction detected. High-wattage devices are strictly prohibited in dormitory zones.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: The core differential equation is... elastoplastic transition... cyclic strength coefficients...

items.speaker: "TARD-E Unit"
items.text: Please comply with the "Startorch Academy Dormitory Code of Conduct"! Issuing first warning. Issuing second warning!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Coefficient correct. Units mismatch? Recalculating...

items.speaker: "TARD-E Unit"
items.text: Noncompliance confirmed. Initiating restraint protocol.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Conclusion: material upgrade required. I'll add it to the agenda for tomorrow's meeting.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Right. Now, time for the scheduled meeting with {PlayerName} and Lynae.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I'm sorry. Did I keep you waiting?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Ah, haha! Not at all. Actually, you noticed us right as the clock struck the hour. Perfect timing, Professor.

options.text: Is the robot going to be okay?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I'm fine.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Just a minor short circuit. It triggered a restraint protocol. I assumed it was malfunctioning.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Oh... I forgot to disable the reactor emitter in my weapon system. That's probably what it was referring to...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Apologies. After this meeting, I need to return to the Spacetrek Collective for a presentation. I had hoped to finish some calculations first.

options.text: If that's the case, let's begin now.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I do have other appointments today, but... I believe there is time for some light conversation.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I wasn't sure what you meant by "delicious," so I brought samples from my latest food research.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: YESSS! I knew it! Professor Mornye's a real gem!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Let's get started! We can eat while we talk!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Of course, come on in. I already tidied up my dorm, so sit wherever you like!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Now, they're back to normal.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: Great! Do you think the data...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It's still there.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But sending the robots out there broke the experiment protocol. Even if you got the data, it won't count in your thesis.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: What?!

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: But... Oh, well. Thanks anyway for helping out. Professor Mornye, and—

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Maja, we do have something we'd like to ask you.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: You want to find a Roya in black robes with gold embroidery, wearing a headpiece shaped like two horns?

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: The horns are a symbol of the Exostrider. <te href=700024>Shepherds</te> wear them, but no Shepherd would wear a robe like that...

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: Could be someone higher up. Like the <te href=700025>Solsworn</te> from the Solisylum, or even one of <te href=700020>the Heliodic Six</te>.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: The Heliodic Six... They're the ones who are supposed to give us the Heliogyre's readings during the New Solar Ceremony.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: That's right. They're the six most powerful members of the Roya tribe. The highest in rank, too.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: I've heard they use the Heliogyre to predict everything, from bird migrations to Void Storms. They know it all.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: How do I meet with one of them?

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: Huh?! That might be hard.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: They never leave the <te href=700018>Solisylum</te>. It's surrounded by light all year round. Very few Roya are even allowed to enter.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: But maybe you could try meeting with one of the unappointed Heliodic Six?

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: Sigrika is currently out researching in the frostlands. The only other one around is Foreseer Yngvar, but he doesn't show himself much.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: If you want to find him, my mam might be able to help. She's a Royan Shepherdess over at Atuja camp. Name's Astrid.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: Now then, I'd better get back to my class before I lose all my credits. Who knew I'd run into trouble today? I even asked my mam to help me with the robot.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Very good. Good luck with your thesis paper.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Phew... I'm better at talking with students in class.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: {PlayerName}, did you learn what you needed?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The last person that those robots likely saw before they lost control was Maja's mother.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I think we can talk to her under the guise of a friendly house visit.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Hmm... In that case, I can accompany you as a Professor from the Academy. As for your identity, {PlayerName}...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It's okay. It will simply be a convenient title.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Would it be okay for you to act as... my mentor?

options.text: You mean, act as a Professor too? Sure.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But aren't you going to tell me I should report this to the Academy to handle?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I was just thinking of ways to help you find who you're looking for.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Your reasons are your own. I trust your judgement, and you can tell me later.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes, feel free to give me pointers too, Mentor.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I think the person I am now... can be of help to you.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: Calm down, Maja, calm down... Good, they've stopped for now. Maybe I should just shut them down?

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: No, if I force the shutdown, I'll lose the data. Our thesis! Oh, somebody help!!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Ugh...

options.text: Is she in a bad situation?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: No. It's just a routine malfunction. But...

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: Huh? Professor Mornye?!

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: Oh no, I've been caught out of class! Wait, wait, let me explain! I wasn't skipping class! I'm here for a very good reason! It's not that I don't respect the class, o-or my teachers!

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: So, uh... can you just...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Even if you beg me, credits will still be deducted.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Why don't you start by explaining yourself?

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: Well, these are probe robots from a few days ago. We sent them out to measure Voidmatter concentrations in the tundra.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: We thought we'd finish collecting the data before class, but when we tried to approach them, they started attacking us.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: I chased them all the way here. That's why I missed the class today...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: The internal <te href=700023>Exoswarm material</te> was eroded by Voidmatter. That's what caused the malfunction.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: This should have been covered in your Material Science coursework. Such material is inherently unstable and can only be used in controlled environments.

items.speaker: Maja
items.text: I'm so sorry...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: For safety reasons, you'll need to shut down the units first, then clean the contaminated parts with Voidmatter.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But approaching them right now may lead to further damage. We need a pulse generator to stun them from a distance. I'll submit a request.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: No wait, that's against protocol...

items.speaker: Probe Robots
items.text: ￥#&@... Scanning... Target... Eradication...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Never mind. I'll think about how to write the review report later.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Looks like it was this.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: With this shut down, the Void Storm is gone.

options.text: Was that one of your Resonance Abilities?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Eh? No, I–I just read a lot about this...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: From its structure, it looks like a canister that can both store and emit energy.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ugh, it's so stinky!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: It smells just like that giant worm you fought! Didn't we deal with it already?!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Giant worm... You mean the <te href=700004>Voidworm</te>?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I see. It's capturing frequencies with high precision, storing energy data.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It's based on ecological models, tracking activity in natural environments, and reconstructing that data mathematically.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: A field modulator, parametric projection...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Oh, sorry.

options.text: Can you try a version that I can understand?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Right. Simply put, this device extracted the Voidworm's frequency and used itself as the epicenter to spread Voidmatter. That storm just now was artificially created.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Maja's probe got contaminated because they likely made contact with it by accident.
Chuuu...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Eh? Did you guys just hear something?
Chuuu… Chuuuu…

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It's coming from under that heap of Exoswarm material. Let's go take a look.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: You two. Please halt.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: From your clothing, I see of my people you are not. What brings you to Haldarheim?

items.speaker: Soliskin
items.text: Chuuu!

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Oh? A Soliskin...

items.speaker: Soliskin
items.text: Chuuu! Chuuu!

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Heh. How interesting... Soliskin trust only those they feel safe with. Its trust you have earned. My thanks for guiding it back.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: But I must ask, I sense one of you carries the aura of the Spacetrek Collective. The other, from the vast world beyond the frostlands. Why come under the guise of Shepherds?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Not sure who here knows what Yngvar did. Best not to mention his name. Don't want to stir things up.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: We rescued this Soliskin from a Void Storm. We were told it needed to return here for the Rite of Return.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Void Storm? You mean, the one just before?

items.speaker: Soliskin
items.text: Chuuu—

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Haha! Then grateful I must be to you both!

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: But ask I must—the Void Storm, it happened near Atuja camp, yes?

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: A Shepherdess named Astrid abides there. Saved the stray Soliskin you did, yet entrust it to a true Shepherdess you did not. Why come all this way?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I forgot about that. Without a solid reason, he might start to suspect something.)

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Because... Astrid has been hiding something from you.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Your meaning?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Astrid's daughter, Maja, is a student at Startorch Academy. She conducted experiments that broke protocol and caused damage to school property.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Maja is now facing harsh punishment. Astrid is busy handling the consequences and cannot fulfill her duties as a Shepherdess.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: According to the Startorch Academy Faculty Code, as Maja's teachers, it is our duty to address the repercussions of a student's actions.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Since Astrid can't shepherd this Soliskin, we must step in to do so ourselves.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: I should have known Astrid's daughter would stir such trouble! A tangled weave this time, but she will go to great lengths for her girl.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: The Academy's willingness to come this far is unexpected. Forgive my earlier words. You saved the Soliskin and helped our people. The title of "Shepherd," you have earned.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Tonight, we hold a feast for tomorrow's <te href=700028>Rite of Return</te>. Join us, as a gesture to make amends for my earlier rudeness.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Come. From this day forth, the Roya call you friends.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Rest at the camp if you wish. The feast will begin at the eighth hour of the night.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Friends—it is time we feast!

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Tomorrow marks the Rite of Return, where we send our beloved Soliskin back to the great body of the Exostrider. Into radiant light they will transform to join its sacred form.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Farewell it will be, yet a new beginning as well! For when the Exostrider wakes, we will reunite in its body!

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: Now, let us put aside the sorrow of parting and embrace the joy of new life with good food and drink!

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: But heed this, my friends. Too much drink you will not have. Should you make a fool of yourself in tomorrow's Rite, help I will not give.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: We will face Foreseer Yngvar tomorrow.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I've simulated seventy-three possible outcomes, but even so, I can't cover every scenario.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: If I could build a model that covers all possible outcomes, considering the target system, the chaotic conditions, and the necessary compensations...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You were quick on your feet earlier. You've already got this.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: That's because I believed your approach was effective. I tried to follow along.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Did I really do well?

options.text: Of course. Give yourself more credit.
Yes. Keep practicing and it'll come more easily.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Good. Phew...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Understood. I'll make sure to do better next time.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: With that in mind, you can relax a bit now, right?

items.speaker: Soliskin
items.text: Chuuu! Chuuu-chuuu!

items.speaker: Soliskin
items.text: Chuuu, chuuu!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ah, I know! It wants us to give it a name.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Well, since it's really trying to buddy up to us, how about we call it "Buddy"?

items.speaker: Soliskin
items.text: Chuuu!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Oh? It seems to like it.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Then it's settled.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Well... that's a relief.

items.speaker: Dagna
items.text: Hello, will you dance with us and the Soliskin?

items.speaker: Stian
items.text: Grandfather Aksel says the Soliskin are happiest when dancing with Shepherds!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Oh, but I'm not good at dancing.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'll join you. Let's dance with Buddy. What do you say?

items.speaker: Children
items.text: HOORAY!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Our real battle starts tomorrow.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Tonight, let's relax a little, Mornye.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes. My mentor should take {Male=his;Female=her} own advice, too.

items.speaker: Distressed Soliskin
items.text: Chu... Chu... Uuuugh...
Hmph...
Some uninvited guests have arrived.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Mentor?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: We can drop the act now, right? Or do you think we should continue?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Someone might be listening. We should keep it up to prevent us from being exposed.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Do you... want to sit for a while?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Oh, you noticed.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: They've been running all day, so their power is low. I planned to wait until they finished charging before coming back.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: While they charge, I can't move. I feel a bit, er... embarrassed having you see me like this.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It doesn't anymore.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Before I had prosthetic legs, I had a condition that kept me in a wheelchair.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Now, at least I don't have to worry about being immobile anymore.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I can finally chase after what I really want.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Mm, I love stars.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Beneath Lahai-Roi's frostlands, the stars here are actually just lights created by atmospheric arrays.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Once, someone put it like this to me...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: "It's because we see the stars that humanity imagines what lies beyond the sky."

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: "Maybe the stars are humanity's muse."

options.text: An interesting way to put it.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I think so too.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But... I also believe that reaching the stars isn't enough.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: There will be a day we build a miracle that lasts longer and is more beautiful than the stars themselves.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: That's the dream I've carried all this time. And now, I'll use these two legs to take the first step.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Exactly! This ceremony is how we retrieve the Exostrider's core energy component.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Then we'll use the multiphasic energy conversion model through the superconducting network to inject Rabelle energy into the neural interface.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It will follow a three-stage activation protocol. The reverse-phase oscillation model, then excavation to finally create a phased-array energy field.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: There's still some time before my legs finish charging. Let's stargaze a little longer.

items.speaker: Buddy
items.text: Chu, Chuuu! Chuuu! Chuuu!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Okay, little Buddy. I'll wake {Male=him;Female=her} up now.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Mentor, you're awake!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Something isn't right. We seem to have a situation in Bjartr Woods.

dialog.stepLabel: Mornye: "Just like before. When it's disabled, the Void Storm stops...."

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Just like before. When it's disabled, the Void Storm stops.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: However...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: If this really was the work of Foreseer Yngvar, what would make him do something like this?
Do not worry. The path... has already been laid.
At last... finally...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Mentor? Mentor! Are you alright?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Did you see something again? It seemed worse than last time.

options.text: I think I saw a glimpse inside Solisylum.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: This Void Storm might not have just affected the woods here, but the workings of Solisylum itself.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I think that's why I've been seeing visions of this member of the Six.

items.speaker: Elderly Shepherd
items.text: Greetings.

items.speaker: Elderly Shepherd
items.text: The Shaper once made a prediction. If a visitor from beyond Lahai-Roi protects Bjartr Woods from destruction, an invitation to Solisylum they will receive.

items.speaker: Elderly Shepherd
items.text: Now, the one from the prediction appears. <te href=700018>Solisylum</te> awaits your arrival.

items.speaker: Elder Soliskin
items.text: Chuuu...

items.speaker: Buddy
items.text: Chu... Chu?

items.speaker: Elderly Shepherd
items.text: They have their own missions to complete.

items.speaker: Elderly Shepherd
items.text: The road to Solisylum is here. Please, follow this path.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Mentor, please... wait a moment.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: There's something I want to say to you.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What's the matter, Mornye？

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I really wish I could accompany you to Solisylum. But I think it's best if we part ways here.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes. When you touched the Solistree earlier, and even during your first vision, you looked like you were in intense pain.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But I lack the ability to connect with them like you do. And the Spacetrek Collective's knowledge doesn't align with the ways of the Roya. In this matter, I fear... I won't be of much help.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Instead of joining you, I think there are other ways I can contribute—such as researching the unknowns we still face.

options.text: Like the true origins of the Void Storm device.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes. Yngvar should have been present at the Rite of Return, were it not for that Void Storm. It's reasonable to suspect he's the one who planted the device.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Additionally, the environment here is unusual. Investigating these anomalies should help our work here.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: No. You, my mentor, are still far ahead of me.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Well then. I guess this is goodbye for now.

options.text: If you're in any danger, call me.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Mm, you too.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Please take care. I'll see you when we regroup.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Hi, are you Astrid, Maja's mom?

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: Hm? Greetings. Yes, I am. And you two are?

options.text: This is Professor Mornye, Maja's teacher.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes, and this is {PlayerName}, a senior professor at the Academy.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: We've come for a home visit.

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: A home visit...? Has the girl broken any school rules?

options.text: Yes. She severely violated the experiment protocol.
She created a robot that caused significant damage to the Academy.

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: What?! H-How could she...

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: I am deeply sorry! I should have taught her better. Whatever she has done, I offer my apologies to the Academy on her behalf.

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: Is the punishment severe? If compensation is needed, I will pay for it myself—

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Professor Mornye, could you explain how Maja's robot caused the damage?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Y-yes, as my mentor just explained...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: She... failed to follow the proper protocol. She used unstable Exoswarm material in her design, which led the robot to act unpredictably.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I've already spoken to her about the dangers of using materials susceptible to Voidmatter during field research.

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: Voidmatter?

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: What... exactly are you asking? I am sorry, I've not studied at your Startorch Academy. I'm not familiar with your terms.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Her demeanor changed just now. She's hiding something. Maybe more pressure.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: We need to understand the full scope of the robot's malfunction. We understand that this morning, it was you who helped Maja activate the robots and enable auto-run.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes. Exactly what my mentor said.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Please, lead us to the site of the incident. Once we're there, I'll reconstruct the robots' actions from the trace evidence.

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: N-No. We can't. If the robots' path is traced, it will expose that thing—

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: I am deeply sorry! Yes, help Maja activate the robots I did, but I... I can't remember the exact location. Please, you must return—

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: That's a Void Storm. How can this be? The Collective hasn't received any recent warnings.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: We need to ensure the safety of those here first.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Questions can wait. Let's go.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ugh... the smells are all mixing together. It's kinda fishy, and the texture is wet and gooey.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: So it's true, huh? You guys actually made food that tastes awful but keeps you running for three days straight. I thought that was just a joke.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It's a scientific breakthrough using energy-compression tech. It's one of Collective's most iconic achievements.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: {PlayerName}, do you... also not like it?

options.text: If this is what progress tastes like, I want to go back.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes! I knew you would understand.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: In fact, its energy compression efficiency exceeds 95%. That exceeds the benchmarks set by both the New Federation and Huaxu Academy, even nearing the theoretical limit.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: From a scientific standpoint, it's a far greater achievement than taste.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I... see...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I'll... raise the priority level of flavor optimization for the next iteration.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Now, I'd like to review the incident with the Hyvatia Detector.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Lynae, {PlayerName}, can you walk me through exactly how you managed to contain it?

options.text: (Describe how you contained the Hyvatia incident.)

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: To summarize, your method of stopping the Hyvatia included blade slashes, repeated shots with coated rounds, and physical force—stomping, striking—until it was rendered inoperable and immobile.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Did I miss anything?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Uh... Professor Mornye, you're not here to...

options.text: ...Make us pay for it, are you?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I mean, it all happened really fast! We didn't have any other way to stop it!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Besides, we're just students! We couldn't afford that even if we wanted to...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: No. You're misunderstanding.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Different damage mechanisms cause distinct failures, and require specific repair methods.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Damage from certain special Resonance abilities is hard to detect through standard diagnostics without prior data. So, you two did well to suppress it physically.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: During the <te href=700016>New Solar Ceremony</te>, the <te href=700013>Hyvatia Detector</te> is essential in monitoring environmental parameters. I've already passed your report to Professor Anslem. He should complete repairs soon.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: At the upcoming meeting with the <te href=700015>Roya</te>, the Spacetrek Collective will need to present a full restoration plan to keep our partnership intact.

options.text: There's a third party involved in this "New Solar Ceremony"?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes, though it's a long story. Where do I even begin...

items.speaker: "TARD-E Unit"
items.text: SCANNING FOR OFFENDERS! VIOLATION DETECTED: Unauthorized high-wattage device in the dormitory. An offender has assaulted this unit. If sighted, report immediately!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Pfft... Even Professor Mornye isn't safe from TARD-E's wrath, huh?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: No worries, I got this. I'll draw its attention while you two make for it.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Three, two, one—

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Mm.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hmm... the frequency that connected with you was coming from here. But now, I don't smell a thing.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The voice from earlier said it would share the last of the Heliogyre's strength so it wouldn't fade. It must be that Soliskin. Did it eventually vanish here?)

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Did you find what you're looking for?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: No. The trail ends here. But I saw other things in that memory.

options.text: Mornye, do you know of a "Heliogyre"?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: The "<te href=700019>Heliogyre</te>" is one of the most important components of the New Solar Ceremony.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Hyvatia can monitor environmental data, but raw observational data on the Reactor Drive can only be obtained from the Heliogyre—a component of the Exostrider itself.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Only the Roya tribe can discern the Heliogyre's readings. That's why the Collective needs their cooperation during the New Solar Ceremony.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: In that memory, I heard a voice say he would force the Heliogyre to a standstill.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: A standstill?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: You're saying you heard a voice asking for help, saying he would stop the Heliogyre's workings.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Could that be why the meeting was cancelled?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (When I handled the Voidworm, a voice told me to protect the sun. Whoever she is, I don't think she intends harm. She offered to help. For now, I'll do what she suggested.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (And one thing is certain. The one who knows me by my "Arbiter" title needs my help.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Hm. Yet another problem tied to the Research Institute. We shouldn't reveal our plans to them just yet.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I need to find out who he is, Mornye. Do you know any Roya who are familiar with the tribe's inner workings?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Hmm... I do have some Royan students. They might know who he is.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It's class hours at the Academy. Should we head back?

items.speaker: Ovid
items.text: Help! Help! Maja—Maja, she... Someone, please help!

items.speaker: Ovid
items.text: Otherwise, our thesis data will disappear entirely!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Don't worry, {PlayerName}. If you see a student shouting "help" and "thesis" in the same breath, no matter how loudly they scream, it's never really an emergency.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: But Maja is one of the Roya students I mentioned. Since you're looking for her, we might as well see what it's about.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Thank you. You don't need to go any further. I can head back to the Research Institute by myself.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Mm, you too.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: That was... an accident...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Huh?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Something wrong?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It's a notice from the Collective. The Royan delegation canceled the meeting. The ceremony is now on hold. That's odd...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Warren handles Royan communications. I'll ask him what's going on when I'm back at the Institute.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Since that slot is free now, I can use the time to recalibrate...

options.text: What don't you give yourself a little break?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Hm, that isn't on the list.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: The New Solar Ceremony is nearly upon us. It's the culmination of decades of work. Everyone at the Collective is giving their all to realize it.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Last time we met at the Institute, I... we made each other a promise. Do you still remember?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Would you like to attend the New Solar Ceremony?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I… I'd want you there to witness it. The rise of the new sun, and humanity taking their first steps into the future they've built with their own hands.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: We did promise to witness it together, didn't we?

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Yes.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: That's all right. Consider this my official invitation, then.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I hope to attend the ceremony together with you.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I… I'd want you there to witness it. The rise of the new sun, and humanity taking their first steps into the future they've built with their own hands.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: As the ceremony draws near, the Spacetrek Collective will become increasingly occupied. Opportunities to meet like this will be rare. But this promise will still stand.
Before my power is spent, I have little time left.
The only choice now is to force the Heliogyre to a standstill.
The last of its strength I will share with you, so fade you will not. But you must hurry.
Before that, take this memory to {Male=him;Female=her}.
Take it to the one they call "Arbiter."

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: {PlayerName}, what happened? Are you alright?!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I... saw someone's memory.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Whoa, you scared me! And just now, a frequency jumped into your Tacet Mark! It felt like... some kinda connection?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Can you trace where it came from, Abby?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Sniff, sniff, sniff...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I smell it coming from over there!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Good. Let's go check it out.

dialog.stepLabel: Mornye: "So, this device is Foreseer Yngvar's. Mentor, what do you ma..."

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: So, this device is Foreseer Yngvar's. Mentor, what do you make of this?

options.text: She doesn't seem to be lying.
We need to verify this ourselves.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Hmm... I trust your judgement.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: This Foreseer Yngvar is the one Maja mentioned before, the unappointed member of the Heliodic Six. Could it be an attempt to tighten his grip on power?

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: No. That is impossible.

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: Each of <ano=the Solsworn>these successors</ano> is chosen when their Resonance Ability first awakens. Even if someone desired the position, it is impossible to change the appointment.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Hmm... meaning no one can threaten Yngvar's position.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (But he could harbor animosity toward the current Heliodic Six. Yngvar is still our lead to find them.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Things are getting more complicated. A direct approach with Yngvar might not be a good idea.)

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: The Solisylum's people usually abide in the village bordering Bjartr Woods, called Haldarheim.

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: It is the time of the <te href=700028>Rite of Return</te>. The Soliskin will return to the Solistrees and to the Exostrider. As is tradition, Foreseer Yngvar will attend the rite.

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: But you are not Roya. You may not be allowed to witness the rite.

items.speaker: Soliskin
items.text: Uuu...

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Huh? Wait, but we did find this little one here.

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: Is this the Soliskin you saved? Well... Perhaps you may attend as "Shepherds."

items.speaker: Astrid
items.text: Soliskin have been companions to the Roya for hundreds of years. If they hear that you saved one, they will be grateful.

items.speaker: Soliskin
items.text: Chu... Chuuu!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: I've never been this close to a Soliskin before. Please let me know if I'm doing something wrong.

items.speaker: Soliskin
items.text: Chuuu!

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: What is it that brings you here? With the Void Storm's fury upon us, it is no time to enter Bjartr Woods.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: This is a disaster not seen in centuries. We have always believed the <te href=700027>Solistrees'</te> light protects the woods from such storms.

items.speaker: Aksel
items.text: The Rite of Return cannot be done, not in these times. I urge you both to head to safety, quickly!

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Let's investigate this together.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Hmm. Where did Mornye go? I'll send her a <ano=WavesLine>message</ano>.
